name: Reeves County Golf Course
about: Reeves County Golf Course is a public 18-hole facility in Pecos, Texas, featuring a driving range equipped with TrackMan digital golf technology at no extra cost. The course offers GPS-enabled rental carts with 10-inch tablets and hosts various tournaments, leagues, and special events like glow-in-the-dark scrambles. Daily and yearly memberships are available, with special rates for military and veterans, making it a versatile destination for golfers of all skill levels.
why_golfers_like_it: TrackMan digital games available at no extra cost
GPS-enabled rental carts with tablets for immersive rounds
Regular tournaments and leagues for competitive play

food_and_drink.notes: Pizza, hot dogs, nachos, and other snacks available on-site; 19th Hole specials include Ribeye and Pork Chop meals

simulator_hardware.notes: Digital golf system available at driving range at no extra cost, with Power Tee coming soon

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: Club rentals are available, but players are welcome to bring their own equipment.

faq.question: Can I bring outside food or drinks?
faq.answer: Outside food and drinks are not mentioned; on-site food and drinks are available for purchase.

faq.question: How long does an 18-hole round typically take?
faq.answer: The course is open Monday-Sunday, with specific play hours (2 PM-11 PM on Mondays, sunrise-sunset Tuesday-Sunday), but typical round times are not specified.

faq.question: Is the venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the facility hosts junior high and high school tournaments and has a welcoming environment for families.
